key_highlights: Broad market sell-off: Dow fell 600+ points, S&P 500's 9-day win streak ended, Nasdaq declined as AI trade faltered.
Jobs data mixed: ADP private payrolls beat expectations, but JPM warns the upcoming jobs report won't help the Fed's decision-making.
Geopolitical tensions: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire fragile, US-Iran talks stall, oil prices volatile, and US Strategic Petroleum Reserve nears 3-year low.
AI sector divergence: Broadcom beat-and-raise but stock slid; Marvell surged 32% on Nvidia CEO's 'next trillion-dollar company' comment; Meta enters enterprise AI race.
IPO market active: Quantinuum, Sunshine Silver Mining, Liftoff, Innio all priced; SpaceX reportedly targeting $1.77 trillion valuation for debut.
Private credit concerns: Resurgent redemptions noted, but Pimco and others say deterioration is slow and contained.
Bitcoin crash: Massive liquidations as Saylor's Bitcoin machine misfires.
Consumer and housing: Lower rates boost home construction stocks; one million Americans vanished from new-car market signaling middle-class strain.
Energy and commodities: Gold advances on geopolitical uncertainty; copper supply crunch flagged; oil dips on ceasefire hopes but clashes persist.
Corporate earnings and moves: Ulta Beauty, Palo Alto Networks, Workday reported; Alphabet raised $85B for AI; Visa and Mastercard collaborate on stablecoin platform.

sectors.trend: Sharp divergence: AI winners like Marvell and ASML gain, but Broadcom slides despite strong results, and the broader AI trade falters. Meta enters enterprise AI with new business agent. Alphabet's massive $85B raise signals long-term commitment.
sectors.implications: Investors are rotating from high-expectation AI names to those with clearer near-term catalysts. Enterprise AI adoption is accelerating, but valuation discipline is key. The AI infrastructure build-out remains a strong secular theme.

sectors.trend: Robinhood plans to expand advisor business via referrals, AI, and IPOs. Visa and Mastercard collaborate on stablecoin platform. Kraken offers IPO access to retail. Private credit sees resurgent redemptions but deterioration is slow.
sectors.implications: Fintech is evolving toward broader wealth management and blockchain integration. Private credit remains a risk but is manageable. Stablecoin adoption by major card networks could reshape payments.

sectors.trend: Gold advances on geopolitical uncertainty. Oil dips on Israel-Lebanon ceasefire but clashes persist. US SPR nears 3-year low due to Iran war drawdown. Copper supply crunch flagged. M2i Global deal surges past $1B.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical risk premium remains elevated. Energy security concerns are real, especially with SPR depletion. Commodities like gold and copper are attractive hedges. Oil volatility will persist.

sectors.trend: One million Americans vanished from new-car market, signaling middle-class crisis. Starbucks plans to boost afternoon business. Nike's World Cup playbook. Ulta Beauty Q1 earnings released. All-inclusive travel trend heating up.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary spending is bifurcated: lower-end consumers are pulling back, while travel and experiences remain strong. Auto sector faces structural demand issues. Retailers need innovation to drive traffic.

sectors.trend: SpaceX targets $1.77T valuation for IPO. Impulse Space raises $500M Series D. New aerospace IPO fizzled. TransDigm performance compared to peers. Defense spending remains elevated due to global tensions.
sectors.implications: Space and defense are high-growth areas but valuations are stretched. IPO activity signals strong investor appetite, but selectivity is critical. Government contracts and geopolitical risks drive demand.

sectors.trend: Lower rates are helping home construction stocks in the ITB ETF. HELOC and home equity loan rates remain low, encouraging borrowing. Illinois passes $56B budget but Bears deal stalled.
sectors.implications: Falling interest rates are a tailwind for homebuilders and REITs. Home equity extraction could support consumer spending. Regional fiscal health varies.

sectors.trend: Novellia raises $18M Series A. CVS Health stock performance compared to S&P 500. McKesson analyst report. Flesh-eating screwworm returns to US threatening cattle herd.
sectors.implications: Healthcare remains a defensive play with steady demand. Biotech funding continues but is selective. Animal health and food supply risks are emerging.

key_themes: Labor market strength vs. Fed policy dilemma
Private credit resilience amid redemption fears
Enterprise AI adoption and infrastructure build-out
Stablecoin and blockchain integration in payments
Energy security and strategic reserve depletion

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to gold, energy stocks, and defense. Reduce exposure to equities with high Middle East revenue exposure.
Diversify within tech: favor companies with strong fundamentals and clear AI monetization (e.g., Marvell, ASML) over hype-driven names. Use stop-losses on high-beta AI stocks.
Hold TIPS, short-duration bonds, and commodities. Avoid long-duration fixed income. Monitor jobs report and Fed commentary closely.
Reduce exposure to lower-rated private credit funds. Favor senior secured loans and Pimco-style selective credit approach.
Avoid leveraged crypto plays. Hold only small strategic allocations. Monitor stablecoin regulation and institutional adoption.
Favor defensive consumer staples (e.g., Colgate-Palmolive) over discretionary. Short auto sector ETFs. Monitor retail earnings.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil supply disruption and SPR depletion
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase energy sector allocation. Hedge with oil futures or ETFs. Monitor Iran negotiations and US SPR levels.

opportunities.rationale: Nvidia CEO endorsement and strong AI-driven demand position Marvell as a key beneficiary of the AI infrastructure build-out. Stock is gaining momentum.
Geopolitical uncertainty, fragile ceasefires, and potential Fed dovishness support gold prices. Gold advances on safe-haven demand.
Lower interest rates are boosting homebuilder stocks. The ITB ETF is well-positioned for a housing recovery.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider IPO participation in high-quality names (e.g., SpaceX, Quantinuum)
opportunities.rationale: IPO market is active with strong debuts (Sunshine Silver, Liftoff, Innio). SpaceX at $1.77T valuation and Quantinuum offer exposure to space and quantum computing.
ASML is a critical supplier for AI chip manufacturing. Its monopoly in EUV lithography ensures long-term demand from TSMC, Samsung, and Intel.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-beta AI stocks and rotate to quality
defensive_moves.rationale: AI trade is faltering with Broadcom sliding despite strong results. Rotate to names with proven earnings and reasonable valuations like MSFT and AVGO on dips.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase allocation to short-duration Treasuries and TIPS
defensive_moves.rationale: Treasuries rose on optimism about Iran peace accord, but inflation risks remain. Short-duration bonds reduce interest rate risk while TIPS provide inflation protection.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against oil price spikes with energy sector ETFs (XLE)
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical tensions and SPR depletion could push oil higher. Energy stocks offer both upside and a hedge against supply disruptions.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to private credit and increase cash
defensive_moves.rationale: Resurgent redemptions in private credit signal potential liquidity stress. Hold cash or short-term government bonds to deploy during dislocations.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid Bitcoin and leveraged crypto positions
defensive_moves.rationale: Bitcoin crash triggered billions in liquidations. Saylor's strategy is misfiring. Regulatory and volatility risks remain elevated.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets are likely to remain volatile due to geopolitical tensions (Middle East), mixed economic data (jobs report), and AI trade rotation. The Dow's record high contrasts with Nasdaq's decline, suggesting a rotation from growth to value. The S&P 500's 9-day win streak ending signals profit-taking. Oil and gold will stay elevated. The Fed is in a bind: strong jobs data but inflation concerns. Expect choppy trading with a defensive tilt.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Secular trends in AI, enterprise software, and energy security remain intact. The AI infrastructure build-out (Alphabet's $85B raise, Marvell, ASML) will drive long-term growth. Geopolitical risks may subside if Iran talks progress, but structural issues (SPR depletion, private credit) need monitoring. Consumer weakness could weigh on GDP. Overall, a cautiously optimistic outlook with selective sector allocation.
key_catalysts: Monthly jobs report (June 5, 2026) - will influence Fed rate path
US-Iran negotiations and Israel-Lebanon ceasefire durability
Fed meeting in June - any shift in dot plot or forward guidance
Amazon Prime Day (June 23-26) - consumer spending indicator
SpaceX IPO filing and valuation details
AI earnings from Nvidia, AMD, and other chipmakers
Oil inventory data and SPR levels
Private credit redemption trends and defaults
European Central Bank and Bank of Japan policy decisions
watch_list: S&P 500 support at 5,200 and resistance at 5,400
